title: Golden Gate Assembly Protocol
author: N.J. Hillson
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6t4heqw
materials: NEB PNK enzyme is supplied with its PNK buffer and it does not contain the ATP required for the phosphorylation reaction. To simplify the reaction step, use T4 ligase buffer (instead of PNK buffer). T4 ligase buffer provides the appropriate amount (1mM ATP) of phosphate for the phosphorylation reaction.

steps.title: Measure the DNA concentration (ng/ml) of each assembly piece
steps.instruction: Measure the DNA concentration (ng/ml) of each assembly piece.

steps.title: Add 100 ng of the linearized...
steps.instruction: Add 100 ng of the linearized vector backbone and equimolar amounts of the other assembly pieces to a 15 ml total volume assembly reaction mixture as follows: linearized vector backbone (100 ng) + each additional assembly piece (to equimolar with backbone) + 1.5 ml 10X NEB T4 Buffer + 0.15 ml 100X BSA* + 1 ml BsaI + 1 ml NEB T4 Ligase, 2 million cohesive end units / mL + ______dH 2 O to 15 ml NOTE: It is essential to use a High Concentration Ligase * BsaI is only 10% active at 37 C without the addition of BSA.

steps.title: Perform the assembly reaction in a thermocycler as follows
steps.instruction: Perform the assembly reaction in a thermocycler as follows: either (following Engler 2009): 3 min @ 37 C } 4 min @ 16 C } 25 cycles 5 min @ 50 C } 5 min @ 80 C } 1 cycle or, alternatively (modified from Engler 2008) 1 hour @ 37 C 1 cycle 5 min @ 50 C } 5 min @ 80 C } 1 cycle NOTE: If any of the assembly pieces contain an internal BsaI site(s), it would be first preferable to silence the internal BsaI site(s) through point mutation(s); a second option, if the internal BsaI site overhang(s) are not cohesive with the other assembly overhangs, is to adjust the thermocycling parameters to terminate after a ligation step (e.g. skip the final cycle at 50 and then 80 C).

steps.title: Transform 5 ml of the assembly...
steps.instruction: Transform 5 ml of the assembly reaction into 100 ml of competent E. coli and/or run a diagnostic agarose gel to check for successful assembly.

steps.title: Oligonucleotides (oligos) come in tubes (dehydrated)...
steps.instruction: Oligonucleotides (oligos) come in tubes (dehydrated) or as liquid stocks (may only be available in 96-well plate format).

steps.title: De-hydrated oligos will have a specified...
steps.instruction: De-hydrated oligos will have a specified quantity (usually in nanomoles) on the tube. Reconstitute the dehydrated oligos with sterile (or RNAase, DNAase free) water to a 100 µM stock. As a rule of thumb, add (ten times the number of nanomoles of DNA in the tube) µL water to make a 100 µM stock.

steps.title: Set up separate phosphorylation reactions for...
steps.instruction: Set up separate phosphorylation reactions for each forward (FW) and reverse (RV) oligos 100 µM oligo stock 2 µL 10X T4 DNA ligase buffer 2 µL Sterile water 15 µL T4 Polynucleotide Kinase 1 µL Total 20 µL

steps.title: Incubate the reaction mixture at 37...
steps.instruction: Incubate the reaction mixture at 37 C for 1 hour and heat inactivate the T4 PNK at 65 C for 20 minutes. Store the phosphorylated oligos at -20 C till further use.

steps.title: Use fresh T4 ligase buffer (<1...
steps.instruction: Use fresh T4 ligase buffer (<1 year old) and avoid repeated freeze/thaw cycles of the buffer. The DTT present in T4 ligase buffer oxides naturally and its oxidation is accelerated by repeated freeze/thaw cycles or excessive heating.

steps.title: Annealing the phosphorylated FW and RV oligos
steps.instruction: Annealing the phosphorylated FW and RV oligos: FW oligo 5 µL RV oligo 5 µL Sterile water 90 µL Total 100 µL

steps.title: Incubate the phosphorylated oligos at 95 C for 3 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate the phosphorylated oligos at 95 C for 3 minutes. If handling >10 annealing reactions, using a thermocycler is convenient. In that case, choose a program step without a heated lid (to ease removal).

steps.title: Cool the reaction slowly at room...
steps.instruction: Cool the reaction slowly at room temperature for ~ 30 min to 1 hr, check the concentration with a nanodrop and store the annealed products at -20 C.
